instructions.content: Run `pantheon-adr new "<Title>"` to create the next-numbered ADR from the house template.
Run `pantheon-adr list` to show every ADR as number, status, and title.
Run `pantheon-adr supersede <number> "<New Title>"` to mark an old ADR superseded and create a linked replacement.
The next ADR number is max(existing) + 1 and gap-tolerant; never reuse or hand-type a number.
Never rewrite an accepted ADR to change its decision; always supersede it so the history is preserved.
Resolve the ADR directory from --dir, then ADR_DIR, then the docs/adr default.
Never rename or reorder the template headings; keep Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered, and Compliance verbatim and in order.
Never record multiple unrelated decisions in one ADR; split them into separate records.
Always populate Alternatives Considered with pros, cons, and the reason each option was rejected; avoid leaving it empty.
Flip Status from Proposed to Accepted only once the team ratifies the decision.
